findings.criterion: Scenario expresses the required precondition, user action, and observable outcomes for adding a single product via drag-and-drop as specified by the behaviour contract 3.normal.add_single_product_via_drag_and_drop.

evidence.quote: The system should allow users to drag and drop product items(data-testid="product-item-1", data-testid="product-item-2", etc.) into a designated drop area (id="div1", data-testid="drop-area") to add them to the cart.
When the user drags the product with data-testid "product-item-1" and drops it into the cart container with data-testid "drop-area"
Then the cart display should show a product with title "The Essence of JavaScript"

findings.reasoning: The behaviour contract requires (1) product items be draggable into the drop area (data-testid="drop-area") and (2) after drop the cart shows title, unit price, and quantity (quantity = 1). The scenario provides the precondition (page loaded and product visible), the explicit action (drag product-item-1 and drop into data-testid "drop-area"), and asserts the expected user-observable outcomes: title, price, and quantity. The scenario's drop target uses the required data-testid and the Then steps assert the same observable fields required by the contract (title, price, quantity = 1).
